questions.question: According to King Lune’s teaching in Narnia, what fundamentally makes a man a king?
options: His superior military strength
The people’s emotional attachment to him
The law that defines and binds his office
His claim to divine right above all rules
questions.answer: The law that defines and binds his office

questions.question: What central political lesson does Lewis embed in the White Witch’s hundred–year rule over Narnia?
options: Long duration in power automatically creates legitimacy
Totalitarian regimes often normalize secret police and fear
Harsh climates inevitably produce harsh governments
Monarchy is always morally worse than democracy
questions.answer: Totalitarian regimes often normalize secret police and fear

questions.question: Why did the American colonists consider Parliamentary taxation illegitimate, according to the discussion?
options: Because they opposed any form of taxation on principle
Because only the colonial legislatures, not Parliament, had lawful taxing authority over them
Because the tax rate was higher than in England
Because the king personally refused to collect the taxes
questions.answer: Because only the colonial legislatures, not Parliament, had lawful taxing authority over them

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the Reformation doctrine of the lesser magistrate as described in the conversation?
options: To justify private citizens starting revolutions whenever they feel oppressed
To require unconditional obedience to all higher officials
To channel resistance to tyranny through existing lawful authorities within the political structure
To eliminate the need for any armed resistance in Christian societies
questions.answer: To channel resistance to tyranny through existing lawful authorities within the political structure

questions.question: How does the conversation connect civil disobedience to Romans 13 in modern constitutional systems?
options: Breaking unjust laws to gain standing in court can be part of lawful appeal to higher authority
Romans 13 forbids any legal appeals once a law is passed
Civil disobedience is always equivalent to rebellion against God
Appealing laws is only acceptable when a majority votes for it
questions.answer: Breaking unjust laws to gain standing in court can be part of lawful appeal to higher authority

questions.question: Why does Lewis prefer stories like Narnia over pamphlets for shaping political and moral understanding?
options: Stories are easier to censor than pamphlets
Stories endure longer and shape the imagination more deeply than topical arguments
Pamphlets are inherently dishonest while stories are always neutral
Stories avoid any reference to real-world conflicts
questions.answer: Stories endure longer and shape the imagination more deeply than topical arguments

questions.question: What aspect of King Lune’s definition of kingship illustrates sacrificial leadership?
options: Delegating all dangerous tasks to professional soldiers
Being first in desperate attacks and last in desperate retreats
Maintaining strict distance from common people
Ensuring all decisions are made by popular vote
questions.answer: Being first in desperate attacks and last in desperate retreats

questions.question: What political danger does Lewis associate with ideological democracy rooted in Rousseau?
options: It inevitably collapses into monarchy
It concentrates power in an unaccountable technocratic elite
It treats the collective will as infallible and tends toward totalitarianism
It completely abolishes all forms of state power
questions.answer: It treats the collective will as infallible and tends toward totalitarianism

questions.question: How does the transcript portray the relationship between worship and political order in Narnia and Calormen?
options: Religious belief has no real impact on political structures
Worship of Aslan fosters free, limited government, while worship of Tash nurtures slavery and despotism
Both Aslan and Tash worshippers produce identical political systems
Polytheistic worship always leads to more freedom than monotheistic worship
questions.answer: Worship of Aslan fosters free, limited government, while worship of Tash nurtures slavery and despotism

questions.question: What is Lewis criticizing through Eustace’s admiration of Calormen as “the least phony country”?
options: The tendency of progressives to romanticize overtly tyrannical regimes
The idea that children should read fantasy literature
The existence of cultural differences between nations
The use of mythological creatures in political allegory
questions.answer: The tendency of progressives to romanticize overtly tyrannical regimes

questions.question: What is the primary concern Lewis raises about bureaucracy and emergency powers in essays like “A Dream”?
options: That emergencies will end too quickly for governments to respond
That bureaucrats are usually underqualified for their jobs
That temporary wartime controls will be kept permanently, eroding ordinary freedoms
That citizens will refuse to cooperate during any genuine crisis
questions.answer: That temporary wartime controls will be kept permanently, eroding ordinary freedoms

questions.question: How does the conversation characterize aristocracy in Lewis’s thought?
options: As rule by hereditary wealth regardless of character
As a necessary hierarchy of excellence and virtue that should serve those below it
As an outdated system wholly incompatible with Christianity
As a purely symbolic institution with no real responsibilities
questions.answer: As a necessary hierarchy of excellence and virtue that should serve those below it

questions.question: Why does Lewis argue that education which merely “feels democratic” can actually destroy democracy?
options: Because it overemphasizes foreign languages
Because it focuses too much on standardized testing
Because leveling standards to protect feelings undermines excellence needed for responsible citizenship
Because it teaches too much classical literature
questions.answer: Because leveling standards to protect feelings undermines excellence needed for responsible citizenship

questions.question: What is the main rationale in Narnia for women receiving weapons like Lucy’s dagger, according to the discussion?
options: Women are expected to lead all battles by default
Weapons are primarily ceremonial for female characters
In extreme situations, women may need to defend themselves when many men have already fallen
Lewis rejects any distinction between male and female roles in war
questions.answer: In extreme situations, women may need to defend themselves when many men have already fallen

questions.question: What is the key political responsibility of Narnian kings with respect to borders and neighboring lands?
options: To abolish all borders and merge kingdoms
To ensure strict cultural uniformity across all regions
To protect their realm from external threats while allowing local communities to live and let live
To impose Narnian law and worship on every neighboring nation
questions.answer: To protect their realm from external threats while allowing local communities to live and let live
